description: Add shared applyClientCadenceChange(trx, {tenant, clientId, newCycle, newAnchor}) helper that updates the clients.billing_cycle scalar
applyClientCadenceChange updates client_billing_settings anchor (day-of-week/day-of-month/etc) in the same transaction
applyClientCadenceChange refreshes client_billing_cycles active windows to match the new cadence in the same transaction
applyClientCadenceChange re-materializes recurring_service_periods for every client-cadence obligation of the client in the same transaction
Route updateBillingCycle through applyClientCadenceChange (closes the specific gap that broke for IT initiative)
Route updateClientBillingSchedule through applyClientCadenceChange
Route the billing-cycle anchor change actions through applyClientCadenceChange
Audit contract/line sync paths (syncRecurringServicePeriodsForContract*) and converge them on the shared helper, or document why they remain separate
Re-materialization computes regenerationStart as max(obligation start, last billed boundary)
Cadence change is atomic: scalar, cycle windows, and RSP rows all commit or all roll back together
Re-materialization never supersedes rows in lifecycle_state 'billed'
Re-materialization never modifies invoice-linked rows (invoice_id or invoice_charge_detail_id not null)
Unbilled future rows are superseded and replaced with new-cadence rows
Detect when the affected range overlaps billed periods (compute billed boundary for the client)
Expose a 'billed periods preserved' warning flag in the change/preview result when billed periods are in range
Add previewClientCadenceChange action that returns impact without writing any data
Preview reports the count of unbilled periods that will be regenerated
Preview reports the count of contract lines affected
Preview reports the regeneration start date and the new cadence
Preview reports whether billed periods fall in the affected range (warning)
Preview lists the affected schedule keys for a details expander
The billing-cycle control opens a confirmation dialog before applying a change
Dialog shows old to new cadence
Dialog shows unbilled-periods and lines-affected counts from the preview
Dialog shows the regeneration start date
Dialog shows the billed-preserved warning when applicable
Apply button commits the change via applyClientCadenceChange
Cancel button closes the dialog and makes no changes
Add repairAllRecurringServicePeriodsForTenant action that enumerates stale/gapped client-cadence schedules
Repair-all re-materializes each stale schedule, reusing repairScheduleMaterialization
Repair-all preserves billed periods using the same billed-protection rule
Repair-all returns a summary (schedules repaired, rows backfilled/realigned/superseded)
Repair-all is idempotent: a second run on a clean tenant makes no changes
Repair-all requires the recurring-service-period 'regenerate' permission
Replace gap-panel detail text with a plain-language explanation of the cause
Add a 'Fix all' button to the Automatic Invoices gap panel that calls repair-all
'Fix all' shows progress and a result summary
Service Periods tab shows a tenant Repair-all entry point
State labels are accurate after repair (no stale 'repair required' rows remain)
Show a clean empty-state message when no gaps remain
Cadence change remains gated by billing:update permission
Preview and repair remain gated by the existing recurring-service-period permissions
